scope: Glasswing first-party theme source and repository tooling

measuredRules.evidence: Intentional best-effort browser cleanup and compatibility fallbacks keep the primary rendering operation available when optional DOM operations fail.
The theme contract retains callback and renderer signatures supplied by Press even when Glasswing does not consume every argument locally.
The current source has no useless assignments, so the recommended rule remains enabled while the evidence probe guards the measured zero.

noGrowth.policy: Repository tooling keeps every recommended rule enabled. Theme source excludes only the two measured legacy rules; all 37 path, rule, message, full lexical owner, full-file AST path with array ordinals, and normalized diagnostic-context fingerprints must match exactly and may only shrink relative to the merge base. All other recommended diagnostics fail CI.

inlineConfiguration.policy: Source comments cannot disable or reconfigure ESLint rules. The executable proof loads the real project configuration and confirms a used no-undef directive neither suppresses nor downgrades the severity-2 diagnostic.

eslint.removalCondition: Remove an exclusion after an intentional theme cleanup makes its exact diagnostic count reach zero without changing the Press theme contract.

diagnosticBaseline.identity: path, rule, messageId, message, full lexical owner, full-file AST path with array ordinals, normalized diagnostic-context fingerprint, and duplicate occurrence
diagnosticBaseline.bootstrap: The initial baseline must be a subset of diagnostics independently recomputed from the exact merge-base theme source; a same-count owner or context swap is forbidden.

noGrowth.policy: New candidate files must pass Prettier. A changed legacy file must be formatted and removed from the baseline; only exact-content renames may transfer baseline ownership.

htmlSinks.policy: Every known HTML write or parser sink is classified by path, full lexical owner, full-file AST path with array ordinals, kind, semantic token fingerprint, disposition, and rationale. The first policy must be a subset of sinks independently recomputed from the exact merge-base theme source; after that, approved keys may only shrink and established wrapper names cannot be removed or renamed. Wrapper and native sink aliases, binds, calls, exports, callbacks, destructured document references, and dynamic parser MIME values fail closed. Reflect and Object helper aliases, bind/call/apply invocation forms, opaque properties or payloads, const or spread Object.assign payloads, defineProperty, and defineProperties HTML-property writes are explicit sinks.
